ITEM 1.    BUSINESS
Overview
Salesforce is a global leader in customer relationship management ("CRM") technology that brings companies and their customers together. Founded in 1999, we enable companies of every size and industry to take advantage of powerful technologies to connect to their customers in a whole new way and help them transform their businesses around the customer in this digital-first world.
Our Customer 360 platform unites sales, service, marketing, commerce and IT teams by connecting customer data across systems, apps and devices to create a complete view of customers. With this single source of customer truth, teams can be more responsive, productive and efficient, deliver intelligent, personalized experiences across every channel and increase productivity. With Slack, we provide a digital headquarters where companies, employees, governments and stakeholders can collaborate to create success from anywhere.
Our Customer 360 service offerings are designed to be flexible, scalable and easy to use. They can generally be configured easily, deployed rapidly and integrated with other platforms and enterprise applications. We sell to businesses worldwide, primarily on a subscription basis, through our direct sales efforts and also indirectly through partners. We also enable third parties to use our platform and developer tools to create additional functionality and new applications that run on our platform, which are sold separately from, or in conjunction with, our service offerings.
Salesforce is committed to a core set of values: trust, customer success, innovation, equality and sustainability. Foremost among these is trust, which is the foundation for everything we do. Our customers trust our technology to deli
…[truncated 20261 chars]…
consumer application companies that have entered the business software market; and
8
Table of Contents
- traditional platform development environment companies and cloud computing development platform companies who may develop toolsets and products that allow customers to build new apps that run on the customers' current infrastructure or as hosted services.
We believe more companies may become competitive threats due to the accelerated shift to cloud and hosted service offerings and customer experience management solutions. We also expect our competition to change and evolve as we expand into more markets, with new offerings.
Customers
We sell to businesses of all sizes and in almost every industry worldwide. The number of paying subscriptions at each of our customers ranges from one to hundreds of thousands. None of our customers accounted for more than five percent of our revenues in fiscal years 2023, 2022 or 2021. In addition, we do not have any material dependencies on any specific product, service or particular group or groups.
Customer Service and Support
We offer professional services to help customers achieve business results faster with Salesforce solutions. Our architects and innovation program teams act as advisors to plan and execute digital transformations for our customers. This includes implementation services for multi-cloud and complex deployments. We provide best-practices and AI-based recommendations and adoption programs globally. In addition, we provide advanced education, including in-person and online courses, to certify our customers and partners on architecting, administering, deploying and developing our service offerings.
Our global customer support group responds to both business and technical inquiries about the use of our products via the web, telephone, email, social networks and other channels. We provide standard customer support during regular business hours at no charge to customers who purchase any of our paying subscription editions. We also offer premier customer support that is either included in a premium success offering or sold for an additional fee, which can include services such as priority access to technical resources, developer support and system administration. In addition, we offer a premier priority support add-on that includes proactive monitoring, rapid incidence response and instruction from a dedicated support team knowledgeable about the customer's specific enterprise architecture.
Sales and Marketing
We sell our services primarily through our direct sales force, which comprises telephone sales personnel based in regional hubs, field sales personnel based in territories close to their customers and self-service offerings.
To a lesser extent, we also utilize a network of partners who refer sales leads to us and assist in selling to these prospects. This network includes global consulting firms, systems integrators and other partners. In return, we typically pay these partners a fee based on the first-year subscription revenue generated by the customers whom they refer. We continue to invest in developing additional distribution channels for our subscription services.
We use a variety of marketing programs across traditional and social channels to target our prospective and current customers, partners and developers. We focus our marketing activities in the cities and countries with the largest market opportunities. Our primary marketing activities include:
- multichannel marketing campaigns that span email, social media, the web, television and more, which align to a broader customer journey;
- in-person and virtual customer events of all sizes to create customer and prospect awareness, including proprietary events such as Dreamforce and our virtual Dreamforce to You, World Tours and other virtual events, as well as participation in trade shows and industry events;
- live events and original programming on our Salesforce+ streaming service, which includes discussions about the future of technology in the digital-first, work anywhere world and educational content to learn new skills and pursue new career opportunities;
- press and industry analyst relations to garner third-party validation and generate positive coverage for our company, brand, service offerings and value proposition;
- partner co-marketing activities with global and regional implementation partners;
- customer testimonials and our community of Trailblazers: individuals who drive innovation, grow their careers and transform their businesses using the Customer 360 platform;
- in-person and virtual technology event sponsorships; and
- event partnerships with high-profile global brands and organizations.
Intellectual Property
We rely on a combination of trademarks, copyrights, trade secrets and patents and contractual provisions, to protect our proprietary technology and our brands. We also enter into confidentiality and proprietary rights agreements with our employees, consultants and other third parties and control access to software, services, documentation and other proprietary information. We believe the duration of our patents is adequate relative to the expected lives of our service offerings. We also purchase or
9
Table of Contents
license technology that we incorporate into our products or services. At times, we make select intellectual property broadly available at no or low cost to achieve a strategic objective, such as promoting industry standards, advancing interoperability, supporting open source software or attracting and enabling our external development community. While it may be necessary in the future to seek or renew licenses relating to various aspects of our products and business methods, we believe, based upon past experience and industry practice, such licenses generally could be obtained on commercially reasonable terms.
Human Capital Management
Salesforce is committed to a core set of values: trust, customer success, innovation, equality and sustainability. These core values are the foundation of our company culture, which we believe is fundamental to, and a competitive advantage in, our approach to managing our workforce. We believe our company culture fosters open dialogue, collaboration, recognition and a sense of family, all of which allow us to attract and retain the best talent, which is critical for our continued success. For example, our sales, engineering and customer success teams are critical to our ability to grow, innovate and ensure the trust and customer success of our customers.
We believe our efforts in managing our workforce have been effective. Our focus on our workplace environment and a strong company culture has led to recognition across the globe, as evidenced by the following awards: Fortune World's Most Admired Companies (2022 and for the eighth year in a row), Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For (2022 and for the 13th year in a row), Human Rights Campaign Best Places to Work for LGBTQ Equality (2022) and Glassdoor Employees' Choice Best Place to Work in Canada, France, Germany, the United Kingdom and the United States (2022).
As of January 31, 2023, we had 79,390 employees, of which approximately 52 percent were located in the United States and 48 percent were located internationally and approximately 36 percent identified as women, 63 percent identified as men and less than 0.2 percent identified as non-binary. This number includes those individuals impacted by our restructuring plan announced on January 4, 2023, which includes a reduction of our current workforce by approximately 10 percent. None of our employees in the United States are represented by a labor union. However, employees of certain foreign subsidiaries are represented by works councils.
We have continued to invest in equality, diversity and inclusion initiatives, development programs, employee engagement and ongoing communications and feedback. Some of our key human

CRM.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: CRM (Salesforce)**
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01 | Evidence cutoff: 2024-03-28**

---

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory
- **Revenue**: Latest quarter (Q4 FY24, ending 2024-01-31) revenue was $9.29B, up 6.5% QoQ and 10.8% YoY. TTM revenue is $34.86B. Quarterly revenue has grown steadily from $7.41B (Q1 FY23) to $9.29B.
- **Earnings**: Latest net income was $1.45B, with diluted EPS of $1.47. QoQ net income grew 18.1%. YoY EPS growth was -1570.0% (distorted by a negative base). TTM net income is $4.14B. Earnings showed volatility, with a loss in Q4 FY23 (EPS -$0.10) but sharp recovery since.
- **Margins**: Gross margin (latest) was 76.9% ($7.14B/$9.29B). Operating margin was 17.4% ($1.62B/$9.29B). Net margin was 15.6% ($1.45B/$9.29B). Margins expanded significantly from earlier quarters (e.g., operating margin was ~0.4% in Q1 FY23).
- **Cash-Flow**: **Unavailable** for all quarters (explicitly not supplied).
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets grew from $93.02B (Q1 FY23) to $99.82B (Q4 FY24). Total equity increased from $58.88B to $59.65B. Total debt decreased from $10.60B to $9.43B. Debt-to-equity ratio is 0.158.

### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience
- **Earnings Quality**: Earnings volatility is high, with a quarterly loss in Q4 FY23 and sharp swings in EPS ($0.03 to $1.47). Without cash-flow data, we cannot assess cash conversion or non-cash adjustments. Recent margin expansion may indicate improved operational efficiency, but durability is unconfirmed.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Low debt-to-equity (0.158) and stable equity growth suggest strong resilience. Total liabilities increased from $34.15B to $40.18B, but asset growth outpaced this. Equity has remained above $57B throughout.

### 3. Valuation versus Company's Own History
- **P/E Diluted**: Latest is 71.71, within the 365-day range of 70.1–1063.7. Historically, P/E was extreme (e.g., 935.7 on 2023-04-03) due to minimal earnings; it compressed as earnings grew.
- **P/B**: Latest is 4.969, within the range of 3.2–5.4.
- **Net Margin**: Latest is 11.9%, up from 0.7% in early 2023, indicating significant improvement.
- **ROE**: Latest is 6.9%, up from 0.4% in early 2023.
- **Valuation Context**: Current P/E is near the low end of its historical range but remains elevated relative to typical market multiples. Valuation has improved with rising earnings but is not low in absolute terms.

### 4. Price Level and Returns vs. Fundamental Path
- **Price Level**: Latest close is $301.18, near the 52-week high of $318.71 and above the low of $187.31.
- **Returns**: Quarterly return (last ~63 trading days) was +12.9%. Annual return (last ~252 trading days) was +56.6%. Prior quarters showed volatility: +31.6%, -4.4%, +10.3%.
- **Fundamental Context**: Annual revenue growth of 10.8% and swing from loss to profit support the annual return of 56.6%, but the stock has outpaced earnings growth (YoY EPS growth is distorted). Price has been volatile, tracking quarterly earnings surprises.

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside
- **Upside**: 
  - Sustained revenue growth (10.8% YoY) and margin expansion could drive further earnings growth. If operating margins remain above 17%, net income could increase significantly.
  - Low leverage (debt-to-equity 0.158) provides balance-sheet flexibility.
- **Downside**:
  - High valuation (P/E 71.71) leaves little room for disappointment; any slowdown in growth or margin contraction could trigger a de-rating.
  - Lack of cash-flow data raises uncertainty about earnings quality and sustainability.
  - Stock is near 52-week highs after a 56.6% annual rise, increasing vulnerability to profit-taking.

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion
- **Quantitative Basis**: Strong revenue growth and improving margins support recent earnings momentum, but valuation remains elevated (P/E 71.71) and cash-flow data is absent. Balance-sheet strength mitigates downside risk.
- **Risk/Reward**: The stock has priced in significant fundamental improvement. Upside depends on continued earnings growth to justify the multiple; downside is tied to valuation compression if growth stalls. Without cash-flow validation, earnings durability is uncertain. The quantitative evidence suggests a **neutral risk/reward** at current levels, given the high valuation relative to historical ranges and incomplete cash-flow picture.
CRM.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: Salesforce (CRM)**
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01 | Evidence Window: PIT 2024-02-11 to <2024-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative**
According to the 10-K (filing_date: 2023-03-08), Salesforce is a global leader in customer relationship management (CRM) technology. Its core Customer 360 platform unifies sales, service, marketing, commerce, and IT teams by connecting customer data to create a complete, single view of the customer. The business operates on a multi-tenant cloud architecture and sells primarily through subscriptions. Key growth strategies include expanding relationships with existing customers via cross-selling, increasing geographic and industry-specific reach (e.g., financial services, healthcare), and leveraging its extensive partner ecosystem and marketplace (AppExchange). The company emphasizes values of trust, customer success, and innovation.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Corporate Actions & Capital Allocation (Mar 2024):** Salesforce declared its first-ever quarterly dividend of US$0.40 per share and increased its share buyback program by US$10bn (2024-03-08 web context). This signals a shift towards enhanced shareholder returns.
*   **Financial Performance & Guidance (Mar 2024):** The company reported strong Q4 and FY2024 results, driven by sales of Data Cloud, which was characterized as its fastest-growing product (2024-03-05 web context). Analysts noted a solid fourth-quarter performance and strong outlook for profitability (2024-02-29 web context). EPS was expected to grow 18% YoY due to cost-containment efforts (2024-03-08 web context).
*   **Stock Performance & Sentiment (Mar 2024):** The stock was noted as down 2.5% in the 30 days following its last earnings report (2024-03-29 news). An investment analysis downgraded the stock to a "Hold" rating, citing it as "currently overvalued" with limited upside, suggesting a fair value of $305 (2024-03-23 news).
*   **Legal & Regulatory (Mar 2024):** Salesforce failed to use tech industry immunity to block a lawsuit alleging participation in a sex trafficking scheme, though the judge did dismiss some claims (2024-03-29 news). A federal judge refused to dismiss a class-action lawsuit brought by up to 50,000 employees alleging the company allowed expensive and poorly performing investments in its 401(k) plan, sending it toward trial (2024-03-21 news).
*   **Market Position (Mar 2024):** The company was recognized as the top CRM provider for the 10th consecutive year by International Data Corp. (2024-03-07 web context).

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Litigation:** Outstanding legal challenges include a class-action securities lawsuit related to alleged participation in a trafficking scheme and a major class-action 401(k) lawsuit. These pose potential reputational harm and financial liability (2024-03-29, 2024-03-21 news).
    *   **Valuation & Sentiment:** Recent downgrades and commentary suggest the stock may be viewed as overvalued, potentially limiting upside and making it susceptible to sentiment shifts (2024-03-23 news).
    *   **Competitive Pressure:** The 10-K and recent news note a highly competitive, low-barrier, and evolving market, with threats from traditional software vendors, application developers, and new entrants (10-K; 2024-03-25 news context).
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Financial Strength & Shareholder Returns:** The initiation of a dividend and a substantial buyback expansion demonstrate a commitment to returning capital, supported by strong cash flow and margin expansion (2024-03-08 web context).
    *   **Leadership & Growth Execution:** The #1 CRM market position for a decade, strong Q4 results led by Data Cloud, and a continued focus on platform integration (e.g., Slack) provide confidence in execution (2024-03-07, 2024-03-05 web context).
    *   **Analyst Support:** At least one analyst affirmed a "Buy" rating based on solid performance and a strong demand outlook (2024-02-29 web context).

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Positive, with significant caveats.** The core franchise is robust, evidenced by sustained market leadership and strong recent financial performance, particularly in promising areas like Data Cloud. The strategic shift to include a dividend and large buybacks is a positive development for shareholders, reflecting confidence in cash flow durability. However, material legal overhang exists from two separate class-action suits, and recent equity performance suggests the market is grappling with valuation concerns, leading to at least one downgrade. The business itself remains a secular growth leader, but the stock narrative is currently pressured by legal risk and valuation debates.

*Confirmed Evidence:* All developments cited are directly from the supplied 10-K (dated 2023-03-08) and news/web snippets dated within the specified window.
*Inference:* The assessment of "positive, with significant caveats" is inferred from the juxtaposition of strong operational news (dividend, earnings, market leadership) against the headwinds of litigation and valuation downgrades present in the same evidence window.

The raw web context says 'EPS expected to grow 18% YoY to US$9.7 led by cost-containment efforts.' The $9.7 figure is forward-looking EPS guidance, not confirmed actuals. The quant report's TTM EPS (sum of last 4 quarters: 1.47+1.25+1.28+0.20 = $4.20) is materially below $9.7, so this forward estimate is not validated by supplied fundamentals. pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Salesforce is a market-leading CRM franchise with improving profitability (operating margin expanded to 17.4%, net margin 15.6%) and a new shareholder-return posture (first dividend, $10B buyback expansion). However, the stock trades at a P/E of 71.7x near 52-week highs after a 56.6% annual run, leaving limited margin of safety. Cash-flow data is absent from supplied packs, and material legal overhangs persist. The risk/reward is mildly positive but not compelling enough to warrant a conviction Buy.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Salesforce is a market-leading CRM franchise with improving profitability (operating margin expanded to 17.4%, net margin 15.6%) and a new shareholder-return posture (first dividend, $10B buyback expansion). However, the stock trades at a P/E of 71.7x near 52-week highs after a 56.6% annual run, leaving limited margin of safety. Cash-flow data is absent from supplied packs, and material legal overhangs persist. The risk/reward is mildly positive but not compelling enough to warrant a conviction Buy.
key_factors: Revenue grew 10.8% YoY to $9.29B in Q4 FY24, with TTM revenue of $34.86B, demonstrating sustained top-line momentum in a dominant market position (#1 CRM provider for 10 consecutive years per IDC, 2024-03-07).
Operating margin expanded to 17.4% and net margin to 15.6% in Q4 FY24, up from near-zero levels in early FY23, reflecting meaningful cost discipline and operating leverage.

Valuation is stretched: P/E of 71.7x is near the low end of its 365-day range (70.1–1063.7) but remains elevated in absolute terms; the stock is at $301.18 near its 52-week high of $318.71 after a 56.6% annual return, leaving limited upside.

Cash-flow data is unavailable for all eight supplied quarters, preventing independent verification of earnings quality; the dividend initiation and $10B buyback expansion (2024-03-08) signal management confidence but cannot be validated from supplied fundamentals.

Material legal risks persist: a class-action securities lawsuit related to trafficking allegations (2024-03-29) and a 401(k) class-action heading to trial (2024-03-21) create reputational and financial liability overhang.

ITEM 1. Business
The Walt Disney Company, together with its subsidiaries, is a diversified worldwide entertainment company with operations in three segments: Entertainment, Sports and Experiences.
The terms "Company", "we", "our" and "us" are used in this report to refer collectively to the parent company and the subsidiaries through which businesses are conducted.
Human Capital
The Company's key human capital management objectives are to attract, retain and develop the highest quality talent. To support these objectives, the Company's human resources programs are designed to develop talent to prepare them for critical roles and leadership positions for the future; reward and support employees through competitive pay, benefit and perquisite programs; enhance the Company's culture through efforts aimed at making the workplace more engaging and inclusive; acquire talent and facilitate internal talent mobility to create a high-performing, diverse workforce; engage employees as brand ambassadors of the Company's content, products and experiences; and evolve and invest in technology, tools and resources to enable employees at work.
The Company employed approximately 225,000 people as of September 30, 2023, of which approximately 167,000 were employed in the U.S. and approximately 58,000 were employed outside the U.S. Our global workforce is comprised of approximately 77% full time and 16% part time employees, with another 7% being seasonal employees. A significant number of employees in various parts of our businesses, including employees of our theme parks, and writers, directors, actors and production personnel for our productions are cove

TABLE OF CONTENTS
Content Sales/Licensing and Other
The majority of Content Sales/Licensing revenue is derived from TV/VOD, theatrical and home entertainment distribution. In addition, revenue is generated from music distribution, stage plays and post-production services through Industrial Light & Magic and Skywalker Sound.
The Company also publishes National Geographic magazine, which is reported with Content Sales/Licensing.
TV/VOD Distribution
We license our content to third-party television networks, television stations and other video service providers for distribution to viewers on television or a variety of internet-connected devices, including through other DTC services.
Theatrical Distribution
The Company licenses full-length live-action and animated films to theaters globally. Cumulatively through September 30, 2023, the Company has released approximately 1,100 full-length live-action films and 100 full-length animated films. In the domestic and most major international markets, we generally distribute and market our films directly. In certain international markets our films are distributed by independent companies. In some territories, certain films may be exclusively distributed on our DTC streaming services. During fiscal 2024, we expect to release approximately 15 films, although the ultimate number of releases will depend on when productions resume following the writers/actors' work stoppages.
The Company incurs significant marketing and advertising costs before and throughout the theatrical release of a film in an effort to generate public awareness of the film, to increase the public's intent to view the film and to help generate consumer interest in the subsequent home entertainment and other ancillary markets. These costs are expensed as incurred, which may result in a loss on a film in the theatrical markets, including in periods prior to the theatrical release of the film.
Home Entertainment Distribution
We distribute the Company's film and episodic content in home entertainment markets on DVD and Blu-ray disc, through electronic home video licenses and VOD rentals globally.
Domestically and internationally, we distribute directly to retailers and through independent distribution companies. Electronic formats of our film and episodic content may be purchased through e-tailers such as Apple and Amazon, and MVPDs, such as Comcast and DirecTV, and physical formats are generally sold to retailers, such as Walmart and Target. The Company also operates Disney Movie Club, which sells DVD/Blu-ray discs directly to consumers in the U.S. and Canada.
Distribution of film content in the home entertainment window generally starts within three months after the theatrical release. Electronic formats are typically available approximately four to eight weeks ahead of the physical release. We also license titles to VOD e-tailers concurrent with physical home entertainment distribution.
Distribution of episodic content in the home entertainment window includes electronic sales of season passes that can be purchased prior to, during and after the broadcast season with individual episodes typically available to season pass customers shortly after the initial airing of the show in each territory. Access to individual episodes is also available for electronic purchase shortly after the initial airing in each territory.
Disney Theatrical Group
Disney Theatrical Group develops, produces and licenses live entertainment events on Broadway and around the world. Productions include The Lion King, Frozen, Aladdin and Beauty and the Beast.
Disney Theatrical Group also licenses the Company's IP to Feld Entertainment, the producer of Disney On Ice and Marvel Universe Live!.
Disney Music Group
The Disney Music Group encompasses all aspects of the Company's music commercialization and marketing including: recorded music (Walt Disney Records and Hollywood Records); music publishing; and concerts. Disney Music Group distributes music both physically and digitally and also licenses music throughout the world in various forms of media, including: television; print; gaming; and consumer products.
Equity Investment
The Company has a 30% effective interest in Tata Play Limited, which operates a direct-to-home satellite distribution platform in India.

Linear Networks compete with other networks for carriage by MVPDs. The Company's contractual agreements with MVPDs are renewed or renegotiated from time to time in the ordinary course of business. DIS.quantitative_report:
# Disney (DIS) — Quantitative Report | Decision Date: 2024-04-01

---

## 1. Fundamental Trajectory

**Revenue:** TTM revenue is $88.94B. The latest quarter (Q1 FY24, ending 2023-12-30) posted $23.55B, essentially flat YoY at +0.2% versus $23.51B in Q1 FY23. Over the eight-quarter window, revenue ranged from $19.25B (Q2 FY22) to $23.55B, with no clear upward slope—seasonality and content timing dominate the pattern.

**Earnings:** TTM net income is $4.18B. Latest-quarter EPS of $1.04 is the strongest in the eight-quarter series (+48.6% YoY vs. $0.70). However, four-quarter EPS history reads $1.04, $0.15, −$0.25, $0.69—highly volatile, with one loss quarter (Q3 FY23, net loss of $153M).

**Margins:** Latest-quarter operating income of $3.88B on $23.55B revenue implies a 16.5% operating margin. TTM operating income is $13.70B, yielding a TTM operating margin of 15.4%. Reported net margin stands at 4.7%.

**Cash Flow:** No cash-flow data are supplied for any period. Cash conversion cannot be assessed.

**Balance Sheet:** Total equity has grown steadily from $94.66B (2022-04-02) to $105.50B (2023-12-30), a cumulative gain of 11.5%. Total assets have edged down from ~$204B to $197.77B, suggesting modest deleveraging. Total debt (most recent available, 2023-09-30) is $46.43B; implied debt-to-equity is 0.44× on the latest equity base.

---

## 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings Quality — Low Confidence:**
- Quarterly EPS ranges from −$0.25 to +$1.04 over eight quarters; standard deviation of quarterly EPS relative to the mean is extreme, signaling low earnings predictability.
- Q4 FY23 EPS of $0.15 on $21.24B revenue (implied net margin ~0.3%) followed by Q1 FY24 EPS of $1.04 on $23.55B revenue (implied net margin ~9.1%) represents a 7× sequential swing, inconsistent with revenue moving only +10.9%.
- TTM net margin of 4.7% and ROE of 4.0% are both depressed, indicating that reported earnings are thin relative to the asset and equity base.
- No cash-flow data are available to corroborate whether accounting profits convert to cash.

**Balance-Sheet Resilience — Adequate:**
- Equity base of $105.50B provides a large absolute cushion.
- Consistent quarterly equity growth (eight consecutive increases) suggests retained earnings accumulation, not aggressive leverage.
- Debt-to-equity of ~0.44× is moderate; total liabilities ($92.27B) are below total equity.

---

## 3. Valuation vs. Supplied History

| Metric | Current (2024-03-28) | 1-Year Range | Assessment |
|---|---|---|---|
| P/E (diluted) | **53.7×** | 33.9× – 77.0× | Upper half of range |
| P/B | **2.13×** | 1.4× – 2.1× | **At the top of the range** |
| P/S | **2.53×** | n/a (no range) | — |
| Earnings yield | **1.9%** | n/a | Extremely low |
| Net margin | 4.7% | 0.0 – 0.1 (range) | Mid-range |
| ROE | 4.0% | 0.0 – 0.0 (range) | Low |

The P/B ratio of 2.13× sits at the ceiling of its one-year band (1.4–2.1). With ROE at only 4.0%, the market is paying 2.1× book for a business generating a 4% return on that equity—a price-to-book / ROE spread of approximately 53×, implying the market expects substantial future improvement. The P/E of 53.7× on a depressed and volatile earnings base makes the multiple's signal unreliable; even the lowest P/E in the window (33.9×) applied to TTM EPS of ~$2.16 (derived: TTM NI $4.18B / ~1.94B diluted shares from EPS math) would imply a price of ~$73, well below the current $122.36.

---

## 4. Price Level & Return Context

| Period | Price | Next-Period Return |
|---|---|---|
| 2024-03-28 (latest) | **$122.36** | — |
| 2023-12-27 | $90.38 | **+35.4%** |
| 2023-09-27 | $79.90 | +13.1% |
| 2023-06-28 | $88.83 | −10.1% |
| 2023-03-28 | $94.82 | −6.3% |

- **Annual return:** +29.0% (from $94.82 to $122.36).
- **Quarterly return:** +35.4% in the most recent ~63 trading days.
- **52-week range:** $78.73 – $123.74; the stock is within **1.1% of its 52-week high**.
- The prior three quarters showed alternating gains and losses (−6.3%, −10.1%, +13.1%), followed by a sharp single-quarter surge.

**Fundamental-price gap:** The +35.4% quarterly price move occurred against a backdrop of +0.2% YoY revenue growth and +48.6% YoY EPS growth on a low base ($0.70 → $1.04). The P/E expanded from 47.3× (2024-02-26) to 53.7× (2024-03-28) even after incorporating the strong Q1 print—meaning the market bid up the multiple beyond what improved earnings alone justified. P/B expanded from 1.87× to 2.13× in the same month, reaching its ceiling.

---

## 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside

**Upside case (quantitative):**
- If the latest operating margin of 16.5% is sustained (vs. TTM 15.4%) on a flat ~$89B revenue base, TTM operating income could rise from $13.70B to ~$14.7B. Applied to TTM net income, this could push EPS toward ~$1.15+ quarterly, reducing the forward P/E toward the mid-40s from the current 53.7×.
- The equity base has compounded at roughly 5.5% annualized over the eight-quarter window; continued book-value growth supports a rising floor.

**Downside case (quantitative):**
- The stock has rallied 35% in one quarter while TTM ROE is 4.0%, net margin is 4.7%, and revenue growth is +0.2% YoY—there is no proportional fundamental acceleration to sustain the move.
- At 2.13× book, the stock is at the top of its 1-year P/B band. Mean-reversion to the 1-year median P/B (~1.65×) on current book value of ~$54.40/share ($105.50B / ~1.94B shares) would imply a price near **$90**, representing a **−26% decline** from $122.36.
- Quarterly EPS volatility (−$0.25 to +$1.04) means the earnings denominator driving the P/E is unreliable; a single weak quarter could rapidly inflate the P/E further or trigger a de-rating.
- No free-cash-flow data exist to confirm cash generation; if operating earnings are not converting to free cash flow, the equity base growth may be accounting-driven rather than economically meaningful.

---

## 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

The quantitative evidence presents a **skewed risk/reward to the downside** at current levels. The stock is trading at a 52-week-high P/B (2.13×) and an elevated P/E (53.7×) while fundamentals remain mediocre: flat revenue (+0.2% YoY), a 4.0% ROE, 4.7% net margin, and extreme quarterly earnings volatility. The most recent quarter's earnings beat has already been absorbed into a +35.4% price move that far exceeds the revenue and earnings trajectory, expanding multiples rather than compressing them. Cash-flow data are absent, removing a key quality screen. The price has essentially caught up to—and arguably overshot—the fundamental improvement, leaving limited quantitative upside against a reversion to the median valuation range.
DIS.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: The Walt Disney Company (DIS)**
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Disney is a diversified worldwide entertainment company with three primary segments: Entertainment, Sports, and Experiences. The Entertainment segment encompasses linear networks (e.g., ABC, Disney Channel, FX), direct-to-consumer (DTC) streaming services (Disney+, Hulu), and content sales/licensing. The company possesses a significant content library and fuels its ecosystem through franchises like Marvel, Star Wars, and Pixar. The Experiences segment includes its global theme parks and resorts. A key human capital strategy involves investing in employee development and retention through programs like Disney Aspire.

**2. Recent Developments**
*   **Corporate Actions & Legal Settlement (2024-03-28, 2024-03-29):** Disney settled its dispute with Florida Governor Ron DeSantis. The ongoing First Amendment lawsuit ended in a "stalemate," though Disney filed an appeal after a federal judge dismissed its suit (2024-02-27).
*   **Corporate Governance Scrutiny (2024-03-27):** A media industry expert criticized the Disney board for being "too deferential" to CEO Bob Iger, specifically citing the acquisition of 21st Century Fox as "really not a good acquisition."
*   **Business Strategy & Partnerships (2024-03-27):** Disney is pursuing growth in its advertising business through partnerships.
*   **Market Sentiment (2024-03-27, 2024-03-29):** Financial media highlighted Disney stock hitting a 52-week high, with one analyst citing a 15% upside due to expected free cash flow growth. The stock was identified as a trending ticker on a financial platform.
*   **DTC Segment Outlook (2024-03-27):** A narrative piece argued Disney's streaming business, with its large scale and advantage in sports, makes it a better buy than Netflix.
*   **Sports Betting Landscape (2024-03-28):** The expansion of legal sports betting to 38 states was noted as a potential market for Disney to explore.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Political and Regulatory Controversy.** Confirmed litigation and conflict with the Florida government regarding a special tax district and First Amendment issues represent operational and reputational risk (evidence from 2024-02-15, 2024-03-29).
*   **Risk: Governance and Strategic Oversight.** Critical external commentary on the board's oversight of the CEO and the value of past major acquisitions (Fox) introduces narrative risk around leadership and capital allocation (2024-03-27).
*   **Risk: Legal Challenges.** Pending lawsuits allege illegal discrimination related to hiring and casting quotas (2024-02-15).
*   **Offset: Franchise Strength and Ecosystem.** The company's core intellectual property and integrated parks/streaming business model are repeatedly cited as foundational strengths.
*   **Offset: Streaming Business Positioning.** Recent narratives position Disney's combination of scale and sports rights as a competitive advantage in the streaming wars (2024-03-27).
*   **Offset: Positive Market Catalysts.** Stock performance and analyst views citing free cash flow growth and advertising partnerships provide a positive near-term narrative (2024-03-27).

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
Based on the point-in-time evidence before April 1, 2024, the qualitative assessment is **Neutral**.

The confirmed positive developments include the resolution (though via stalemate) of a high-profile political feud, perceived strengths in the streaming/sports nexus, and a positive turn in stock market narrative. These are offset by confirmed negative factors: ongoing legal challenges, pointed criticism of board governance and past deal-making, and residual uncertainty from the Florida conflict. The franchise and ecosystem provide a strong foundation, but the recent narrative is a mix of strategic progress, market enthusiasm, and unresolved governance and legal questions.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Total debt (most recent available, 2023-09-30) is $46.43B; implied debt-to-equity is 0.44× on the latest equity base.' The D/E calculation uses 2023-09-30 debt ($46.43B) against 2023-12-30 equity ($105.50B), mixing two different balance-sheet dates. Using the same-date pair (2023-09-30: debt $46.43B, equity $103.96B) yields D/E of ~0.45×, which is close but the cross-date methodology is imprecise and should be flagged as approximate.
corrections.required_action: Note that the D/E figure is approximate due to mismatched balance-sheet dates; no material change to the conclusion.

corrections.issue: Quant report derives TTM EPS as '$2.16 (derived: TTM NI $4.18B / ~1.94B diluted shares from EPS math).' The share count of ~1.94B is inferred rather than sourced from the raw packs. The raw packs do not provide a diluted share count. The derived figure is plausible given the math ($4.18B / $2.16 ≈ 1.94B), but the report presents it as if it were a known input. This is a minor transparency issue.
corrections.required_action: Acknowledge the share count is back-calculated from TTM NI and EPS, not independently sourced.

corrections.issue: Qual report cites 'Disney Aspire' as a human-capital offset, and the 10-K text confirms this program. However, the qual report does not mention the NBCU put right on Hulu (exercised November 2023), which is a material near-term capital-allocation event disclosed in the 10-K business section. This omission could affect the risk assessment.
corrections.required_action: Consider the Hulu buyout obligation (NBCU's put right) as a material balance-sheet and cash-flow risk not captured in the qualitative risk section.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Disney's stock has rallied 35% in one quarter to within 1% of its 52-week high, pushing P/B to the top of its 1-year range (2.13x) and P/E to 53.7x, while fundamentals remain mediocre: flat revenue (+0.2% YoY), 4.0% ROE, and extreme quarterly earnings volatility. The qualitative picture is mixed—strong franchises and streaming positioning offset by governance concerns, legal risks, and a material Hulu buyout obligation. At current levels, the risk/reward is roughly balanced but skewed slightly negative on valuation stretch.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Disney's stock has rallied 35% in one quarter to within 1% of its 52-week high, pushing P/B to the top of its 1-year range (2.13x) and P/E to 53.7x, while fundamentals remain mediocre: flat revenue (+0.2% YoY), 4.0% ROE, and extreme quarterly earnings volatility. The qualitative picture is mixed—strong franchises and streaming positioning offset by governance concerns, legal risks, and a material Hulu buyout obligation. At current levels, the risk/reward is roughly balanced but skewed slightly negative on valuation stretch.
key_factors: Valuation at ceiling: P/B of 2.13x is at the top of its 1-year range (1.4x–2.1x) and P/E of 53.7x is elevated, with an earnings yield of only 1.9%—the market has priced in substantial improvement that fundamentals have not yet delivered.

Fundamental mediocrity: TTM revenue of $88.94B grew only +0.2% YoY, ROE is 4.0%, net margin is 4.7%, and quarterly EPS is highly volatile (ranging from -$0.25 to +$1.04 over eight quarters), making the earnings base unreliable for valuation.

Price-fundamental disconnect: The +35.4% quarterly price surge occurred alongside +0.2% revenue growth and +48.6% EPS growth on a low base, with P/E expanding from 47.3x to 53.7x—multiple expansion, not earnings growth, drove the rally.

Material unaddressed risk: The NBCU put right on Hulu (exercised November 2023) represents a significant near-term capital-allocation obligation that was omitted from the qualitative risk assessment and could pressure free cash flow and balance-sheet flexibility.

IBM.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Equity Report: IBM (Decision Date: 2024-04-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
- **Revenue**: TTM revenue = 61.86B. Q4 2023 revenue = 17.38B, up 4.1% YoY (Q4 2022: 16.69B) and 17.8% QoQ (Q3 2023: 14.75B).
- **Earnings**: TTM net income = 7.50B. Q4 2023 EPS diluted = 3.56, up 19.1% YoY (Q4 2022: 2.99) and 93.5% QoQ (Q3 2023: 1.84). TTM EPS (sum of last four quarters) = 8.13.
- **Margins**: Q4 2023 gross margin = 59.1% (10.27B / 17.38B), operating margin = 21.6% (3.76B / 17.38B), net margin = 18.9% (3.29B / 17.38B). TTM operating margin = 14.0% (8.69B / 61.86B), TTM net margin = 12.1% (7.50B / 61.86B).
- **Cash Flow**: No cash flow data available.
- **Balance Sheet**: Total equity = 22.61B (2023-12-31) vs. 22.02B (2022-12-31). Total assets = 135.24B vs. 127.24B. Total liabilities = 112.63B vs. 105.22B. Liabilities-to-equity ratio = 4.98 (112.63 / 22.61).

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality**: Quarterly EPS shows volatility: loss of -3.54 in Q3 2022, ranging to 3.56 in Q4 2023. Without cash flow data, cash conversion cannot be assessed.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: High leverage (liabilities/equity = 4.98) with a small equity base (22.61B) relative to assets (135.24B). This increases financial risk.

**3. Valuation vs. Supplied History**
- Current PE diluted = 23.459 (2024-03-28), within the 365-day range of 19.3 to 73.4. P/B = 7.787, near the top of its range (5.1 to 8.1). Earnings yield = 4.3% (1/23.459). ROE = 33.2%.
- Historical PE was as high as 73.367 (2023-04-03) when net margin was 0.027. Current valuation reflects improved profitability but remains above the lowest PE (19.3).

**4. Price Level & Return Context vs. Fundamentals**
- Price: 190.96 on 2024-03-28. Annual return (252 trading days) = +47.6% (from 129.34 on 2023-03-28). Quarterly return (63 trading days) = +16.8% (from 163.46 on 2023-12-27).
- Fundamentals: Q4 2023 YoY revenue growth of 4.1% and EPS growth of 19.1% are positive but much lower than the 47.6% annual price return, indicating significant multiple expansion and sentiment shift.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside**: Continued margin improvement could sustain earnings growth. The PE is not stretched relative to its recent high, leaving room for upside if growth persists.
- **Downside**: High leverage (liabilities/equity = 4.98) amplifies risk if earnings deteriorate or interest rates rise. The sharp price appreciation (+47.6% annually) may have priced in much of the good news.

**6. Provisional Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The fundamentals show improving earnings and margins, but balance-sheet leverage is a material risk. Valuation is moderate on a PE basis but elevated on P/B. The strong price run has outpaced revenue growth, reflecting market optimism. Risk/reward appears balanced: upside requires sustained earnings growth, while downside is heightened by leverage and potential earnings volatility.
IBM.qualitative_report:
**IBM Qualitative Assessment Report**
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
IBM is portrayed as a legacy technology corporation undergoing a strategic transformation. The narrative centers on its pivot from traditional IT infrastructure and services toward growth engines in **hybrid cloud and artificial intelligence (AI)**. Its franchise is characterized by deep enterprise relationships, a broad portfolio of software and consulting services, and emerging investments in areas like quantum computing (2024-02-27). Recent commentary suggests its strategy of integrating Red Hat with its software and consulting offerings is beginning to yield financial improvement, positioning it as a differentiated player in enterprise AI and hybrid cloud solutions.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Corporate Narrative & Market Sentiment (Bullish):** Multiple articles from late March 2024 highlight a positive narrative shift, with headlines stating IBM's "lost decade" is over (2024-03-23) and that the company "succeeded where investors thought it would fail" (2024-03-23). The stock is described as a "no-brainer bargain" (2024-03-30) and a trending stock (2024-03-29).
*   **AI & Growth Catalysts:** A Bank of America analyst note on 2024-03-12 claims IBM has "underappreciated AI potential" and is repositioned for substantial growth. Analyst reports (2024-03-28) and web context (2024-02-17) indicate expectations for modest revenue and EPS growth in 2024, citing performance across business segments and double-digit increases in annual bookings.
*   **Corporate Action:** IBM is actively using its patent portfolio offensively, as evidenced by a patent infringement lawsuit filed against Airbnb (2024-03-28).
*   **Capital Allocation/Buffett Association:** IBM is listed among the AI stocks owned by Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway (2024-03-31), which is presented as a positive sentiment indicator.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Legal & Regulatory:** IBM faces ongoing litigation risks, including the cited patent infringement suit (2024-03-28), age discrimination claims from former employees (2024-02-22), and involvement in data privacy litigation related to its datasets (2024-03-07).
    *   **External Environment:** The company is exposed to global economic volatility, regulatory challenges across countries, trade disputes, and unpredictable customer demand (2024-02-28).
    *   **Competitive Execution:** The turnaround narrative hinges on successful execution of its hybrid cloud/AI strategy against formidable competitors like Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet (2024-02-17).
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Strategic Repositioning:** The consistent narrative across sources is that IBM's focused strategy on hybrid cloud and AI is gaining traction and is seen as a key growth driver (2024-03-12, 2024-02-17, 2024-02-28).
    *   **Financial Improvement:** Commentary points to improved financial performance, including adjusted EPS growth and revenue stabilization, supporting the turnaround thesis (2024-02-17).
    *   **Sentiment & Valuation:** The stock is receiving positive attention and is framed by some analysts as undervalued ("bargain") in a rising market (2024-03-30).

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral to Slightly Positive.** The supplied evidence indicates a meaningful positive shift in IBM's narrative from a stagnating legacy player to a company with a credible growth strategy in hybrid cloud and AI, supported by improving financial metrics and bullish analyst sentiment. However, this positive trajectory is counterbalanced by persistent and varied legal/regulatory risks and the fundamental challenge of achieving significant revenue growth in a highly competitive market. The assessment is tempered by the absence of detailed financial filings in this evidence set to corroborate the claimed operational improvements. The balance of catalysts (strategic focus, analyst upgrades) and risks (execution, litigation) results in a cautiously optimistic but not definitive qualitative view.

*Note: This report is based solely on the provided text snippets and excludes any financial data or external analysis. All points are attributed to specific dated evidence.*

corrections.issue: The qualitative report states IBM is 'listed among the AI stocks owned by Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway (2024-03-31)' and presents this as a positive sentiment indicator. However, the raw evidence article (2024-03-31) only says 'Warren Buffett Has $159 Billion Invested in These 8 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks' and 'Buffett owns more AI stocks than you might think.' The article does not explicitly confirm IBM is among those 8 stocks — the qualitative report may be overstating what the source says, or the source may list IBM but the summary provided does not confirm it. This is a minor sourcing concern.
corrections.required_action: PM should note that the Buffett-IBM ownership link is asserted by the qualitative report but the supplied evidence summary does not explicitly confirm IBM is among the 8 stocks. Treat this as unverified sentiment rather than confirmed ownership.

corrections.issue: The quantitative report states 'Historical PE was as high as 73.367 (2023-04-03) when net margin was 0.027.' The raw valuation data confirms PE=73.367 on 2023-04-03 and net_margin=0.027 on that date. However, the report implies a causal link (low margin caused high PE) without noting that the low net margin in that period was likely driven by the Q3 2022 operating loss of -4.50B (EPS=-3.54) rolling through TTM calculations. The report does not flag that the Q3 2022 loss was a one-time or unusual item — the raw data shows a sharp swing from -3.54 EPS in Q3 2022 to +1.53 in Q2 2022 and +2.99 in Q4 2022, suggesting a discrete charge. This context matters for assessing whether current PE normalization is sustainable.
corrections.required_action: PM should be aware that the PE compression from ~73 to ~23 was largely driven by the roll-off of a large Q3 2022 loss from TTM earnings, not purely organic margin improvement. This affects the durability of the current PE level.

corrections.issue: The qualitative report describes IBM as a 'no-brainer bargain' (2024-03-30) and frames analyst sentiment as bullish. However, the quantitative report notes that the stock has returned +47.6% annually while revenue grew only 4.1% YoY in Q4 2023, indicating significant multiple expansion. The qualitative report does not adequately flag this valuation tension — it presents the 'bargain' narrative without cross-referencing the price appreciation already captured. The two reports are internally somewhat contradictory: quant flags stretched price vs. fundamentals while qual leans bullish on sentiment.
corrections.required_action: PM should weigh the quant report's caution on multiple expansion against the qual report's bullish sentiment framing. The 'bargain' label from one article conflicts with the quant finding that price has far outpaced fundamental growth.

unsupported_claims: The qualitative report states IBM is 'listed among the AI stocks owned by Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway (2024-03-31)' — the raw evidence summary for that article says 'Buffett owns more AI stocks than you might think' but does not explicitly list IBM as one of the 8 stocks in the supplied summary text.

The qualitative report references 'age discrimination claims from former employees (2024-02-22)' — the raw evidence confirms SCOTUS declined to hear ex-IBM workers' age bias claims, but the report frames this as ongoing 'claims' when the evidence indicates the Supreme Court skipped the bid, potentially ending that avenue. The risk characterization may be slightly overstated relative to the supplied text.

- The qualitative report references 'age discrimination claims from former employees (2024-02-22)' — the raw evidence confirms SCOTUS declined to hear ex-IBM workers' age bias claims, but the report frames this as ongoing 'claims' when the evidence indicates the Supreme Court skipped the bid, potentially ending that avenue. The risk characterization may be slightly overstated relative to the supplied text.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: IBM's improving earnings trajectory and credible hybrid cloud/AI narrative support a mild positive view, but the stock's +47.6% annual price appreciation has already captured much of the good news, leaving limited risk-adjusted upside from current levels.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: IBM's improving earnings trajectory and credible hybrid cloud/AI narrative support a mild positive view, but the stock's +47.6% annual price appreciation has already captured much of the good news, leaving limited risk-adjusted upside from current levels.
key_factors: Q4 2023 EPS diluted of 3.56, up 19.1% YoY, with TTM EPS of 8.13 and net margin improving to 12.1%, supporting the earnings recovery narrative.
Stock price of 190.96 reflects +47.6% annual return versus only 4.1% YoY revenue growth in Q4 2023, indicating significant multiple expansion that limits further upside.
High balance-sheet leverage with liabilities-to-equity ratio of 4.98 amplifies downside risk if earnings momentum stalls or interest rates remain elevated.
Hybrid cloud and AI strategy gaining traction per analyst commentary (Bank of America, 2024-03-12), but execution against Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet remains a material competitive risk.

  filing_date=2023-09-07 section=business period_end=2023-07-29
Item 1. Business
General
Cisco designs and sells a broad range of technologies that power the Internet. We are integrating our product portfolios across networking, security, collaboration, applications and the cloud to create highly secure, intelligent platforms for our customers' digital businesses. These platforms are designed to help our customers manage more users, devices and things connecting to their networks. This will enable us to provide customers with a highly secure, intelligent platform for their digital business.
We conduct our business globally and manage our business by geography. Our business is organized into the following three geographic segments: Americas; Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA); and Asia Pacific, Japan, and China (APJC).
Our products and technologies are grouped into the following categories: Secure, Agile Networks; Internet for the Future; Collaboration; End-to-End Security; Optimized Application Experiences; and Other Products. In addition to our product offerings, we provide a broad range of service offerings, including technical support services and advanced services. Increasingly, we are delivering our technologies through software and services. Our customers include businesses of all sizes, public institutions, governments, and service providers, including large webscale providers. These customers often look to us as a strategic partner to help them use information technology (IT) to differentiate themselves and drive positive business outcomes.
We were incorporated in California in 1984 and reincorporated in Delaware in 2021. Our headquarters are in San Jose, California. The mailing address of our headquarters is 170 West Tas
…[truncated 20318 chars]…
ross our security portfolio focused on cloud-based offerings, AI-driven threat detection and end-to-end security architectures. Additionally, we continue to invest in expanding our SASE architecture by delivering combined network and security functionality in a single cloud-native service.
5
Table of Contents
Optimized Application Experiences
The Optimized Application Experiences product category consists of our full stack observability and network assurance offerings. Our full stack observability offerings are designed to bring together and provide end-to-end visibility of our customer's environments across applications, networks, multi-cloud infrastructures and the Internet, to help deliver full stack observability for modern environments and drive relevant real-time insights. Our Intersight Infrastructure Services, a cloud-delivered SaaS offering, provides lifecycle operations management, automation, orchestration, and monitoring capabilities for customers' infrastructure deployments from Data Center to the Edge. Our monitoring and analytics offering, AppDynamics, monitors performance across different application-related domains. Our network assurance offering, ThousandEyes, is a network intelligence platform that provides in-depth visibility into network and Internet performance. It enables organizations to see, understand, and improve every digital experience and assure seamless connectivity for their modern digital environments.
Services
In addition to our product offerings, we provide a broad range of service and support options for our customers. Our overall service and support offerings are combined into one organization, Customer Experience, that is responsible for the end-to-end customer experience.
Our support and maintenance services help our customers ensure their products operate efficiently, remain available, and benefit from the most up-to-date system and application software. These services help customers protect their network investments, manage risk, and minimize downtime for systems running mission-critical applications.
We also provide comprehensive advisory services that are focused on responsive, preventive, and consultative support of our technologies for specific networking needs. We are investing in and expanding advisory services in the areas of software, cloud, security, and analytics, which reflects our strategy of selling customer outcomes. We are focused on three priorities: utilizing technology advisory services to drive higher product and services; assessment and migration services providing the tools, expertise and methodologies to enable our customers to migrate to new technology platforms; and providing optimization services aligned with customers' business expectations.
Customers and Markets
Many factors influence the IT, collaboration, and networking requirements of our customers. These include the size of the organization, number and types of technology systems, geographic location, and business applications deployed throughout the customer's network. Our customer base is not limited to any specific industry, geography, or market segment. Our customers primarily operate in the following markets: enterprise, commercial, service provider, and public sector.
Enterprise
Enterprise businesses are large regional, national, or global organizations with multiple locations or branch offices. Many enterprise businesses have unique IT, collaboration, and networking needs within a multivendor environment. We offer service and support packages, financing, and managed network services, primarily through our service provider partners. We sell these products through a network of third-party application and technology vendors and channel partners, as well as selling directly to these customers.
Commercial
The commercial market represents larger, or midmarket and small businesses. We sell to our midmarket customers through a combination of our direct sales force and channel partners. These customers typically require the latest advanced technologies that our enterprise customers demand, but with less complexity. Small businesses require information technologies and communications products that are easy to configure, install, and maintain. We sell to these smaller organizations within the commercial market primarily through channel partners.
Service Providers
Service providers offer data, voice, video, and mobile/wireless services to businesses, governments, utilities, and consumers worldwide. The service provider market includes regional, national, and international wireline carriers, webscale operators as well as Internet, cable, and wireless providers. We also include media, broadcast, and content providers within our service provider market, as the lines in the telecommunications industry continue to blur between traditional network-based, content-based and application-based services. Service providers use a variety of our products and services for their own networks. In addition, many service providers use Cisco data center, virtualization, and collaboration technologies to offer managed or Internet-based services to their business customers. Compared with other customers, service providers are more likely to require network design, deployment, and support services because of the greater scale and higher complexity of their networks, whose requirements are addressed, we believe, by our architectural approach.
6
Table of Contents
Public Sector
The public sector market includes federal, state and local governments, as well as educational institution customers. Many public sector customers have unique IT, collaboration, and networking needs within a multi-vendor environment. We sell to public sector customers through a network of third-party application and technology vendors, channel partners, as well as through direct sales.
Sales Overview
As of the end of fiscal 2023, our worldwide sales and marketing functions consisted of approximately 26,000 employees, including managers, sales representatives, and technical support personnel. We sell our products and services both directly and indirectly through a variety of channels with support from our salesforce. A substantial portion of our products and services is sold indirectly through channel partners, and the remainder is sold through direct sales. Channel partners include systems integrators, service providers, other third-party resellers, and distributors.
Systems integrators and service providers typically sell directly to end users and often provide system installation, technical support, professional services, and other support services in addition to network equipment sales. Systems integrators also typically integrate our products into an overall solution. Some service providers are also systems integrators.
Distributors may hold inventory and sell to systems integrators, service providers, and other third-party resellers. We refer to sales through distributors as our two-tier system of sales to the end customer. Revenue from two-tier distributors is recognized based on a sell-in method. These distributors may be given business terms that allow them to return a limited portion of inventory, receive credits for changes in selling prices, receive certain rebates, and participate in various cooperative marketing programs.

CSCO.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: CSCO (Decision Date: 2024-04-01)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**  
- **Revenue**: Quarterly revenue grew from $12.84B (Q3 2022) to a peak of $15.20B (Q4 2023), then declined to $14.67B (Q1 2024) and $12.79B (Q2 2024). Q2 2024 revenue is down 12.8% QoQ and 5.9% YoY.  
- **Earnings (EPS diluted)**: Peaked at $0.96 (Q4 2023), fell to $0.89 (Q1 2024) and $0.65 (Q2 2024). Q2 2024 EPS is flat YoY but down 27.0% QoQ.  
- **Margins**:  
  - Gross margin: Stable around 64% (Q2 2024: 64.3%).  
  - Operating margin: Volatile, dropped to 24.2% in Q2 2024 from 29.2% in Q1 2024.  
  - Net margin: Fell to 20.6% in Q2 2024 from 24.8% in Q1 2024.  
- **Cash-flow**: Data unavailable for all periods.  
- **Balance sheet**: Total assets increased from $92.80B (Q3 2022) to $101.17B (Q2 2024). Total equity grew from $40.40B to $46.25B. Total debt decreased from $9.40B to $7.70B.  

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**  
- **Earnings quality**: Margin volatility and the sharp revenue decline in Q2 2024 raise concerns about near-term stability. However, net margin has remained above 20% for all quarters shown.  
- **Balance-sheet resilience**: Debt-to-equity ratio is low at 0.166. Equity growth and debt reduction strengthen financial flexibility.  

**3. Valuation versus History**  
- **PE diluted**: Current 15.1x (2024-03-28) is near the bottom of its 365-day range (14.4x–20.7x).  
- **Price-to-book**: Current 4.4x is near the bottom of its range (4.2x–5.6x).  
- **Net margin**: Current 23.5% is slightly above the historical range (20%–20%).  
- **ROE**: Current 29.1% is slightly below the historical range (30%–30%).  

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context**  
- **Price**: Latest close $49.91 (2024-03-28). 52-week range: $45.55–$58.19.  
- **Returns**: Stock is down 1.0% over the past year and 1.1% over the past quarter, despite the fundamental deterioration in Q2 2024. The stock declined 5.2% from Sep to Dec 2023 and has been relatively flat since.  

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**  
- **Upside**: Valuation multiple expansion (e.g., PE returning to 17–18x) if earnings stabilize. Strong balance sheet provides resilience.  
- **Downside**: Continued revenue and earnings decline (Q2 2024 revenue -12.8% QoQ). Annualized Q2 2024 EPS of $2.60 is significantly below TTM EPS of $3.30, implying potential further de-rating if trends persist.  

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**  
The stock trades at historically low valuation multiples (PE 15.1x, near 365-day low) but faces deteriorating fundamental trends (revenue and earnings declining sharply in the latest quarter). Balance-sheet strength mitigates downside, but the absence of cash-flow data limits assessment of cash generation. Risk/reward is balanced: low valuation offers upside if fundamentals stabilize, but continued weakness could lead to further pressure.
CSCO.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Research Report: Cisco Systems (CSCO)**
**Report Date: 2024-04-01**

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Cisco is a global technology leader designing and selling a broad portfolio that powers the internet. Its core business is organized into product categories including **Secure, Agile Networks** (core switching/routing/wireless), **Internet for the Future** (routed optical networking, Silicon One), **Collaboration** (Webex), and **End-to-End Security**. A strategic priority is the ongoing **transformation to a software- and subscription-based model** to provide more flexible consumption via offerings like Cisco Plus (networking-as-a-service). The company serves enterprise, commercial, service provider, and public sector customers globally through direct and indirect channels. Its stated strategy is to "securely connect everything," with key customer priorities around infrastructure transformation, security, and hybrid work.

### 2. Recent Developments (Pre-2024-04-01)
*   **Major Corporate Action (Acquisition):** On **2024-03-18**, Cisco **closed its acquisition of Splunk**, significantly ahead of schedule. Analysts and news reports frame this as a "game changer" for growth and free cash flow, with Cisco securing approximately half a quarter of Splunk's contribution in its fiscal third quarter (Piper Sandler analyst note, 2024-03-20).
*   **Product Launches:** On **2024-03-26**, Cisco unveiled new hybrid work devices, including the **Board Pro G2 and Desk Phone 9800 Series**, expanding its hardware footprint in the hybrid workforce domain.
*   **Market & Analyst Sentiment:** On **2024-03-20**, Piper Sandler raised its price target on CSCO from $47 to $51 while maintaining a Neutral rating, citing the Splunk acquisition. Earlier, on **2024-02-21**, web context noted analyst expectations for adjusted EPS growth of 4% in fiscal 2025, which would miss the company's long-term goal of 5%-7% growth.
*   **Security & Market Awareness:** On **2024-03-27**, Cisco published a study indicating that very few organizations in Canada are prepared for the evolving cyber threat landscape, highlighting a market need relevant to its security portfolio.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
*   **Risk: Integration & Execution from Splunk.** The SEC filing from **2024-03-18** explicitly lists risks related to Cisco's ability to successfully integrate Splunk's "market opportunities, technology, personnel and operations and to achieve expected benefits." This is a major near-term execution risk.
*   **Risk: Competitive and Cyclical Headwinds.** Web context from **2024-02-21** states that Cisco "faces fierce competition" and that "most of Cisco's problems seem cyclical." This suggests challenges in sustaining growth in its core networking markets.
*   **Risk: Legal.** On **2024-02-14**, Cisco was reported to have been hit with a patent infringement lawsuit concerning network processing technology, representing ongoing litigation risk.
*   **Offset: Expanded Software & Services Opportunity.** The Splunk acquisition (**2024-03-18**) immediately and significantly bolsters Cisco's software revenue, observability, and security analytics capabilities, directly supporting its strategic business model transformation.
*   **Offset: Continued Product Innovation.** The launch of new hybrid work devices (**2024-03-26**) demonstrates continued investment in product cycles within a key strategic area (hybrid work).

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral, with a Positive Near-Term Catalyst.**

The qualitative narrative is dominated by the **early and transformative closure of the Splunk acquisition (2024-03-18)**, which provides a clear, tangible catalyst for shifting Cisco's revenue mix toward higher-growth software and could accelerate its strategic transformation. However, this is balanced by confirmed risks of integration execution, fierce competition, and cyclical pressures in its core networking hardware business. The new product launches are incremental positives. The overall stance is Neutral, leaning Positive in the immediate term due to the materiality of the Splunk catalyst, pending evidence of successful integration and offset to cyclical headwinds.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Net margin: Current 23.5% is slightly above the historical range (20%–20%)' and 'ROE: Current 29.1% is slightly below the historical range (30%–30%)'. The raw valuation data shows net_margin range 0.2..0.2 and roe range 0.3..0.3, which are single-point ranges (likely rounding artifacts). The quant report's phrasing 'slightly above' and 'slightly below' implies meaningful deviation from a band, but the ranges are effectively single values (20% and 30%). This is misleading framing rather than a factual error, but could overstate the significance of the deviation.
corrections.required_action: Clarify that the historical ranges for net_margin and ROE are essentially flat (single-point ranges), so the current values are not meaningfully 'above' or 'below' a range but rather consistent with historical levels.

corrections.issue: Qual report cites 'Cisco Profits Are Up 29%' from a 2024-03-29 Network Computing article and attributes a quote to CEO John Chambers about 'sixth quarter of sequential growth.' This web context item appears to reference an older period (John Chambers was CEO until 2015; Chuck Robbins has been CEO since). The raw evidence does not provide enough detail to confirm this is current or relevant to the 2024 period. The qual report does not directly use this claim, but it sits in the evidence pack without disambiguation.
corrections.required_action: No action required in the report itself, but note that the web context item dated 2024-03-29 referencing John Chambers likely pertains to archived/legacy content and should not be treated as current evidence.

unsupported_claims: Quant report: 'Q2 2024 EPS is flat YoY' — Raw data shows Q2 2024 EPS=0.65 vs Q2 2023 EPS=0.67, which is -3.0% YoY, not flat. The growth panel confirms eps_diluted YoY = -3.0%.

Quant report: 'Net margin: Current 23.5% is slightly above the historical range (20%–20%)' — The raw valuation data shows net_margin range 0.2..0.2 (i.e., 20% to 20%), a single-point range. The current 23.5% is actually well above this range, not 'slightly above.' The sparse history shows net_margin values from 0.209 to 0.235, suggesting the range in the raw data may be truncated/rounded. The quant report's characterization is inconsistent with its own cited data.

Quant report: 'ROE: Current 29.1% is slightly below the historical range (30%–30%)' — Same issue: the raw range is 0.3..0.3 (single point). The sparse history shows ROE values from 0.271 to 0.300, so 29.1% is within the actual observed range, not below it.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Cisco trades at historically low valuations (PE 15.1x near 365-day low) with a strong balance sheet (D/E 0.166), but faces deteriorating fundamentals—Q2 2024 revenue down 12.8% QoQ and EPS down 27% QoQ. The transformative Splunk acquisition (closed 2024-03-18) provides a meaningful catalyst for software revenue acceleration, but integration execution risk and cyclical headwinds in core networking temper conviction.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Cisco trades at historically low valuations (PE 15.1x near 365-day low) with a strong balance sheet (D/E 0.166), but faces deteriorating fundamentals—Q2 2024 revenue down 12.8% QoQ and EPS down 27% QoQ. The transformative Splunk acquisition (closed 2024-03-18) provides a meaningful catalyst for software revenue acceleration, but integration execution risk and cyclical headwinds in core networking temper conviction.
key_factors: Q2 2024 revenue declined 12.8% QoQ to $12.79B and EPS fell 27% QoQ to $0.65, signaling meaningful fundamental deterioration in the core business.
PE of 15.1x sits near the bottom of its 365-day range (14.4x–20.7x), and price-to-book of 4.4x is also near its low, providing valuation support if earnings stabilize.
Splunk acquisition closed 2024-03-18, described as a 'game changer' for software revenue and free cash flow, with ~half a quarter of contribution expected in fiscal Q3.
Balance sheet remains resilient with D/E of 0.166, total debt reduced from $9.4B to $7.7B, and equity growing to $46.25B, providing financial flexibility to absorb integration costs.

Item 1.BUSINESS
General
Johnson & Johnson and its subsidiaries (the Company) have approximately 152,700 employees worldwide engaged in the research and development, manufacture and sale of a broad range of products in the healthcare field. Johnson & Johnson is a holding company, with operating companies conducting business in virtually all countries of the world. The Company's primary focus is products related to human health and well-being. Johnson & Johnson was incorporated in the State of New Jersey in 1887.
The Executive Committee of Johnson & Johnson is the principal management group responsible for the strategic operations and allocation of the resources of the Company. This Committee oversees and coordinates the activities of the Company's three business segments: Consumer Health, Pharmaceutical and MedTech (previously referred to as Medical Devices). Of note is the increased enforcement activity by data protection authorities in various jurisdictions, particularly in the European Union, where significant fines have been levied on companies for data breaches, violations of privacy requirements, and unlawful cross-border data transfers.  In the U.S., the Federal Trade Commission has stepped up enforcement of data privacy with several significant settlements and there have been a material increase in class-action lawsuits linked to the collection and use of biometric data.
Further, the Company relies on global supply chains, and production and distribution processes, that are complex, are subject to increasing regulatory requirements, and may be faced with unexpected changes such as those resulting from the
